summary: 45-45-90 and 30-60-90 triangles have fixed side ratios, so knowing just one side gives you all three instantly.
explanation: A 45-45-90 triangle is an isosceles right triangle: the two legs are equal, and the hypotenuse is always \(\sqrt{2}\) times a leg. A 30-60-90 triangle has sides in the fixed ratio \(x : x\sqrt{3} : 2x\), where \(x\) is the side opposite the 30° angle (the shortest side), \(x\sqrt3\) is opposite the 60° angle, and \(2x\) (the hypotenuse) is opposite the 90° angle. These ratios let you find every side from just one given side, without needing the Pythagorean theorem at all. These triangles show up constantly in GRE geometry — half of a square (cut along a diagonal) is a 45-45-90 triangle, and half of an equilateral triangle (cut by an altitude) is a 30-60-90 triangle.
